question: He was known for his ______ approach to problem-solving, always finding the most practical solution.
definition: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically in a way that is based on practical rather than theoretical considerations.

question: The beauty of a sunset is ______, lasting only for a few fleeting minutes before the dark sets in.
definition: Lasting for a very short time.

question: In today's digital age, smartphones have become ______, seen in the hands of almost everyone on the street.
definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.

question: The ______ salesman kept talking for an hour, barely giving anyone else a chance to speak.
definition: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative.

question: She was incredibly ______ in her accounting work, double-checking every single receipt and ledger entry.
definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.

question: His ______ attention to his appearance meant he never left the house with even a single wrinkle in his shirt.
definition: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail.

question: The politician tried to ______ the issue by giving a long, confusing answer that avoided the main point.
definition: To render obscure, unclear, or unintelligible.

question: To ______ the effects of the drought, the city implemented strict water conservation rules.
definition: To make less severe, serious, or painful.

question: Scratching the mosquito bite will only ______ the itch and make it redder.
definition: To make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse.

question: Despite the harsh winter, the ______ plants in the garden bloomed beautifully in the spring.
definition: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.

question: Her ______ mood swings made it difficult for her friends to know how she would react to any given situation.
definition: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior.

question: The professor's lecture on quantum mechanics was so ______ that only a few graduate students understood it.
definition: Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with specialized knowledge.

question: Stephen King is a ______ author, having written and published dozens of bestselling novels.
definition: Producing much fruit, foliage, or many offspring; (of an artist/author) producing many works.

question: The billionaire's ______ display of wealth included gold-plated cars and diamond-encrusted watches.
definition: Characterized by vulgar or pretentious display; designed to impress or attract notice.

question: After weeks of thrilling travel, returning to her ______ office job felt incredibly boring.
definition: Lacking interest or excitement; dull.

question: How the ancient pyramids were built remains an ______ to many modern historians and engineers.
definition: A person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand.

question: Voter ______ was high this year, resulting in the lowest election turnout in a decade.
definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern.

question: The instructions were so ______ that I wasn't sure whether to turn left or right at the fork.
definition: Open to more than one interpretation; having a double meaning.

question: My brother is a ______ person who loves attending parties and meeting new people.
definition: Fond of company; sociable.

question: Birds have an ______ sense of direction that allows them to migrate thousands of miles without a map.
definition: Inborn; natural.

question: Many cultures ______ their elders, treating them with the utmost respect and seeking their wisdom.
definition: To feel deep respect or admiration for something.

question: Normally outgoing, she was unusually ______ about her new job and refused to share any details.
definition: Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily.

question: The boss was surrounded by ______, employees who constantly agreed with him just to get a promotion.
definition: A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important in order to gain advantage.

question: He approached the haunted house with a sense of ______, his heart pounding in his chest.
definition: A feeling of fear or agitation about something that may happen.

question: New DNA evidence helped to ______ the man who had been falsely imprisoned for twenty years.
definition: Clear someone of blame or suspicion.

question: The ______ sports fans painted their faces and screamed until their voices were hoarse.
definition: Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or an objective.

question: The ______ donor anonymously gave one million dollars to the children's hospital.
definition: Well meaning and kindly.

question: The hacker's ______ software was designed to steal passwords and destroy personal files.
definition: Intending or intended to do harm.

question: After winning the championship three years in a row, the team grew ______ and lost their competitive edge.
definition: Showing smug or uncritical satisfaction with oneself or one's achievements.

question: If you are ______ in your studies, you are much more likely to earn high grades.
definition: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work or duties.

question: The speaker delivered an ______ speech that moved the entire audience to tears.
definition: Fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing.

question: Living a ______ lifestyle allowed them to save up enough money to buy a house in cash.
definition: Sparing or economical with regard to money or food.

question: The ______ waiter looked down his nose at us when we asked for ketchup at the fancy restaurant.
definition: Arrogantly superior and disdainful.

question: His ______ decision to quit his job without having another one lined up caused him a lot of stress.
definition: Acting or done quickly and without thought or care.

question: The art exhibit sought to ______ classical paintings with modern street art to show the contrast in styles.
definition: Place or deal with close together for contrasting effect.

question: After eating the massive Thanksgiving dinner, everyone felt too ______ to get off the couch.
definition: Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic.

question: It was ______ of the winner to praise her opponent's skill during the trophy presentation.
definition: Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.

question: He tried to look ______ about failing the driving test, but we could tell he was deeply upset.
definition: Feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not displaying anxiety, interest, or enthusiasm.

question: Cassette tapes have become largely ______ in the era of digital music streaming.
definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.

question: She sat by the window in a ______ mood, quietly reflecting on the choices she had made.
definition: Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought.

question: His ______ dream of single-handedly ending world poverty was noble, but highly unrealistic.
definition: Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.

question: The phrase 'free gift' is ______, since a gift is inherently free by definition.
definition: Not or no longer needed or useful; superfluous.

question: The board of directors ignored the minor details and focused only on the most ______ points of the presentation.
definition: Most noticeable or important.

question: The police need ______ evidence, like a weapon or fingerprints, to make an arrest, not just rumors.
definition: Perceptible by touch; clear and definite; real.

question: The sheer scale of the hurricane was ______, causing more destruction than any storm in recorded history.
definition: Never done or known before.

question: The stock market can be highly ______, with prices violently rising and falling in a single day.
definition: Liable to change rapidly and unpredictably, especially for the worse.

question: As the night wore on, the partygoers' energy began to ______ and people started heading home.
definition: Decrease in vigor, power, or extent; become weaker.

question: At the ______ of his career, the actor was starring in three blockbuster movies a year.
definition: The time at which something is most powerful or successful.

question: The king was forced to ______ his throne after the successful revolution by the working class.
definition: Renounce one's throne; fail to fulfill or undertake a duty or responsibility.

question: The celebrity was hit with a ______ of questions from the paparazzi the moment she stepped out of the car.
definition: A concentrated outpouring of questions or blows.

question: The elementary school band's first practice sounded like a ______ of squeaking clarinets and banging drums.
definition: A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds.

question: There is a ______ of fresh produce in the desert town, making fruits and vegetables very expensive.
definition: A scarcity or lack of something.

question: The ______ children ran into the amusement park, bubbling over with excitement.
definition: Cheerful and full of energy.

question: I was just being ______ when I said I would eat a hundred hot dogs; I didn't actually mean it.
definition: Treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor.

question: The coach's inspiring halftime speech helped to ______ the losing team into playing their best.
definition: Shock or excite someone into taking action.

question: The movie relied on ______ plot twists that audiences have seen a million times before.
definition: Lacking significance through having been overused; unoriginal and trite.

question: As an ______, the artist constantly challenged traditional artistic conventions and societal norms.
definition: A person who attacks settled beliefs or institutions.

question: The legal document was filled with so much ______ that the average person couldn't understand it.
definition: Special words or expressions that are used by a particular profession or group.

question: The teacher tried to ______ a love of reading in her students by introducing them to fantasy novels.
definition: To light or set on fire; to arouse or inspire an emotion.

question: The sequel to the hit movie was a ______ disappointment, lacking the charm and energy of the original.
definition: Lacking in vitality, force, or conviction; uninspired or uninspiring.

question: The author is famous for his ______ tales of horror, ghosts, and gruesome murders.
definition: Disturbing and horrifying because of involvement with or depiction of death and injury.

question: His constant taking of selfies and talking about himself revealed a deep level of ______.
definition: Excessive interest in or admiration of oneself and one's physical appearance.

question: The wrecking ball was brought in to completely ______ the old, condemned apartment building.
definition: To destroy utterly; wipe out.

question: The invention of the internet created a massive ______ shift in how humanity communicates.
definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model.

question: She was in a ______ over whether to accept the high-paying job she hated, or the low-paying job she loved.
definition: A state of perplexity or uncertainty over what to do in a difficult situation.

question: The disease ran ______ through the overcrowded city before the vaccine could be distributed.
definition: Flourishing or spreading unchecked (especially something unwelcome or unpleasant).

question: The ______ old woman gave the young couple excellent advice about how to maintain a happy marriage.
definition: Having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment; shrewd.

question: The usually ______ teenager shocked his parents by talking non-stop about his new favorite video game.
definition: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.

question: The sudden loud noise in the dark alley was enough to ______ even the bravest police officer.
definition: To make someone lose courage or confidence.

question: He tended to ______ between the two options, unable to make a final, firm decision.
definition: Alternate or waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.

question: The university decided to ______ the application fee for low-income students to encourage them to apply.
definition: Refrain from insisting on or using (a right or claim).

question: The politician's speech was full of ______, blaming immigrants and foreigners for all the country's problems.
definition: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries.

question: After living in the snowy mountains for years, I began to ______ for the warm beaches of my hometown.
definition: Have an intense feeling of longing for something, typically something that one has lost or been separated from.

question: The religious ______ stood on the street corner screaming at passersby about the end of the world.
definition: A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their religious, political, or other ideals.

question: The rock star soaked up the ______ of the screaming fans in the front row.
definition: Excessive admiration or praise.

question: The small town began to ______ into a bustling city once the new tech company built its headquarters there.
definition: Begin to grow or increase rapidly; flourish.

question: The child tried to ______ his mother into buying him candy by giving her extra hugs.
definition: Persuade someone to do something by sustained coaxing or flattery.

question: The younger monks bowed their heads in ______ to the elder abbot of the monastery.
definition: Humble submission and respect.

question: Many young basketball players try to ______ the shooting style of their favorite professional athletes.
definition: Match or surpass (a person or achievement), typically by imitation.

question: The article was based on a ______ premise, leading to an entirely incorrect conclusion.
definition: Based on a mistaken belief.

question: The independent filmmaker hopes to ______ enough support online to fund her next project.
definition: Gather or collect (something, especially information or approval).

question: The sudden drop in barometric pressure is often a ______ of a severe thunderstorm.
definition: A person or thing that announces or signals the approach of another.

question: The dark, swirling clouds indicated that a tornado was ______ and people needed to seek shelter.
definition: About to happen.

question: A ______ buyer will always compare prices and read reviews before making a large purchase.
definition: Having, showing, or done with good judgment or sense.

question: The diplomats worked late into the night trying to untangle the ______ political conflict.
definition: Extremely difficult or intricate.

question: The extreme heat made everyone feel ______, preferring to lie in the shade rather than move around.
definition: Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort; slow and relaxed.

question: He was a ______ in the scientific community, often rejecting accepted theories to pursue his own wild ideas.
definition: An unorthodox or independent-minded person.

question: As a ______ in chess, she frequently fell for simple traps set by her more experienced opponents.
definition: A person new to or inexperienced in a field or situation.

question: The ancient text was filled with ______ references that even the best historians couldn't translate.
definition: Not discovered or known about; uncertain.

question: The new manager believed that free coffee was a ______ for all the company's morale problems.
definition: A solution or remedy for all difficulties or diseases.

question: The riot police used tear gas to ______ the violent uprising in the city square.
definition: Put an end to (a rebellion or other disorder), typically by the use of force.

question: Despite their messy divorce, the two parents tried to raise their child without any ______ toward each other.
definition: Bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing.

question: The detectives had to ______ the security footage frame by frame to identify the license plate.
definition: Examine or inspect closely and thoroughly.

question: The superhero arrived just in time to ______ the villain's evil plan to destroy the city.
definition: Prevent (someone) from accomplishing something.

question: The wheat fields seemed to ______ in the gentle breeze, looking like ocean waves.
definition: Move or go with a smooth up-and-down motion.

question: She made a ______ denial of the accusations, shouting her innocence for everyone to hear.
definition: Showing strong feeling; forceful, passionate, or intense.

question: The ______ fox managed to sneak past the guard dogs and steal a chicken from the coop.
definition: Skilled at gaining an advantage, especially deceitfully.

question: The invention of the printing press was a ______ moment in human history, changing education forever.
definition: An event or period marking a turning point in a course of action or state of affairs.

question: The general refused to ______ to the enemy's demands, vowing to fight to the last man.
definition: Surrender or submit to another's authority.

question: The gentle ______ blowing through the open window provided a welcome relief from the summer heat.
definition: A soft gentle breeze.

question: The storm will ______ by morning, allowing us to travel safely.
definition: Become less intense or widespread.

question: His angry outburst was an ______, as he is usually very calm.
definition: A departure from what is normal or expected.

question: I ______ violence of any kind and refuse to watch action movies.
definition: Regard with disgust and hatred.

question: The refugees lived in ______ poverty, lacking even basic shelter.
definition: Extremely bad, unpleasant, and degrading.

question: The priest agreed to ______ the man of his sins.
definition: Set or declare someone free from blame or guilt.

question: During the diet, she decided to ______ from eating chocolate entirely.
definition: Restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something.

question: The professor's ______ philosophical theories confused most of the class.
definition: Difficult to understand; obscure.

question: The food at that restaurant is so ______ that I will never return.
definition: Extremely bad; appalling.

question: The scientist received an ______ for her groundbreaking cancer research.
definition: An award or privilege granted as a special honor.

question: Reporters will often ______ celebrities in the street to get a quote.
definition: Approach and address someone boldly or aggressively.

question: The movie critic was known for his ______ reviews that ruined careers.
definition: Sharp and forthright; tasting sour or bitter.

question: I did not want to go, but I had to ______ to my boss's demands.
definition: Accept something reluctantly but without protest.

question: The burning plastic produced an ______ smoke that burned our eyes.
definition: Having an irritatingly strong and unpleasant taste or smell.

question: Their divorce was highly ______, filled with shouting and bitter arguments.
definition: Angry and bitter.

question: Her business ______ allowed her to turn a small startup into an empire.
definition: The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions.

question: Dogs have an ______ sense of smell, much stronger than humans.
definition: Present or experienced to a severe or intense degree.

question: He was ______ that he was right, refusing to change his mind.
definition: Refusing to be persuaded or to change one's mind.

question: She is a strict ______ to the rules of her religion.
definition: Someone who supports a particular party, person, or set of ideas.

question: The teacher had to ______ the student for talking during the exam.
definition: Warn or reprimand someone firmly.

question: He was an ______ negotiator, easily convincing others to agree with him.
definition: Clever or skillful in using the hands or mind.

question: Greedy merchants used to ______ the flour by mixing it with cheap chalk.
definition: Render something poorer in quality by adding another substance.

question: The medicine had several ______ side effects, including nausea and headaches.
definition: Preventing success or development; harmful.

question: The new building has a modern ______ that appeals to younger buyers.
definition: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.

question: The new neighbor was very ______ and quickly made friends with everyone.
definition: Friendly, good-natured, or easy to talk to.

question: His fake British accent was just an ______ to make him seem wealthy.
definition: Behavior, speech, or writing that is artificial and designed to impress.

question: She felt a strong ______ for the stray dog and immediately adopted him.
definition: A spontaneous or natural liking or sympathy for someone or something.

question: The ______ businessman owned three mansions and a private jet.
definition: Having a great deal of money; wealthy.

question: The country had an ______ economy based mostly on farming and agriculture.
definition: Relating to cultivated land or the cultivation of land.

question: She accepted the job offer with ______, thrilled to start working.
definition: Brisk and cheerful readiness.

question: The prosecutor will ______ that the defendant stole the money.
definition: Claim or assert that someone has done something illegal or wrong.

question: Applying ice to the injury will help ______ the swelling and pain.
definition: Make suffering, deficiency, or a problem less severe.

question: The government will ______ one million dollars to the new school project.
definition: Distribute resources or duties for a particular purpose.

question: He was an ______ teenager who preferred to sit alone rather than socialize.
definition: Not friendly or forthcoming; cool and distant.

question: Volunteering at the homeless shelter is an act of pure ______.
definition: The belief in or practice of disinterested and selfless concern for the well-being of others.

question: The two competing software companies decided to ______ into one massive corporation.
definition: Combine or unite to form one organization or structure.

question: The candlelight and soft music gave the restaurant a romantic ______.
definition: The character and atmosphere of a place.

question: He felt ______ about the move, excited for the new job but sad to leave his friends.
definition: Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something or someone.

question: The new manager sought to ______ the terrible working conditions in the factory.
definition: Make something bad or unsatisfactory better.

question: My parents were usually ______ to letting my friends sleep over on weekends.
definition: Open and responsive to suggestion; easily persuaded or controlled.

question: The ______ hostess made sure every guest at the party felt welcome.
definition: Having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner.

question: The newly elected president granted ______ to all political prisoners.
definition: An official pardon for people who have been convicted of political offenses.

question: He wrote an ______ poem for his wife on their anniversary.
definition: Showing, feeling, or relating to sexual desire.

question: The slime was an ______ blob that lacked any specific shape.
definition: Without a clearly defined shape or form.

question: Seeing a smartphone in a movie set in the 1920s is a glaring ______.
definition: A thing belonging or appropriate to a period other than that in which it exists.

question: The teacher used an ______ to explain how the human heart is like a water pump.
definition: A comparison between two things, typically for the purpose of explanation.

question: When the government collapsed, the country descended into total ______.
definition: A state of disorder due to absence or nonrecognition of authority.

question: The grandfather told a funny ______ about his childhood on the farm.
definition: A short and amusing or interesting story about a real incident or person.

question: She cried out in ______ when she heard the terrible news.
definition: Severe mental or physical pain or suffering.

question: There was a long history of ______ between the two rival families.
definition: Strong hostility.

question: Finding a penguin in the desert would certainly be an ______.
definition: Something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected.

question: The typewriter is the historical ______ to the modern computer keyboard.
definition: A thing or event that existed before or logically precedes another.

question: Cats and dogs are often said to have a natural ______ toward one another.
definition: A deep-seated feeling of dislike; aversion.

question: His cowardly actions were the exact ______ of bravery.
definition: A person or thing that is the direct opposite of someone or something else.

question: Many teenagers view politics with pure ______, not caring who wins the election.
definition: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern.

question: My grandmother frequently repeated the ______ 'a penny saved is a penny earned'.
definition: A pithy observation that contains a general truth.

question: The zombie movie depicted the ______ of modern civilization.
definition: The complete final destruction of the world.

question: The story about George Washington chopping down the cherry tree is likely ______.
definition: Of doubtful authenticity, although widely circulated as being true.

question: To ______ the crying child, the mother bought him a toy.
definition: Pacify or placate someone by acceding to their demands.

question: I felt very ______ about flying in an airplane for the first time.
definition: Anxious or fearful that something bad or unpleasant will happen.

question: The mayor's plan met with the general ______ of the city council.
definition: Approval or praise.

question: The manager decided to ______ company funds to pay for his personal vacation.
definition: Take something for one's own use, typically without the owner's permission.

question: She has a natural ______ for mathematics, solving complex equations easily.
definition: A natural ability to do something.

question: The judge served as the final ______ in the legal dispute.
definition: A person who settles a dispute or has ultimate authority in a matter.

question: The teacher's grading system seemed entirely ______, based on mood rather than merit.
definition: Based on random choice or personal whim, rather than any reason or system.

question: The ancient wizard studied ______ spells that few living people understood.
definition: Understood by few; mysterious or secret.

question: Using a floppy disk to store data is completely ______ in the 21st century.
definition: Very old or old-fashioned.

question: Superman is the classic ______ of a comic book hero.
definition: A very typical example of a certain person or thing.

question: He was an ______ supporter of environmental protection, protesting every weekend.
definition: Enthusiastic or passionate.

question: Climbing Mount Everest is an physically ______ task that takes months of training.
definition: Involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring.

question: The royal family's ______ manners made them seem out of touch with regular people.
definition: Of, belonging to, or typical of the aristocracy; grand and stylish.

question: The ______ lawyer managed to find a loophole that kept his client out of jail.
definition: Clever or skillful, typically in a crafty or cunning way.

question: The magician's entire show was an ______ designed to fool the audience's eyes.
definition: Clever or cunning devices or expedients, especially as used to trick or deceive others.

question: The rebel army quickly gained ______ over the weakened government forces.
definition: Occupation of a position of dominant power or influence.

question: The monk lived an ______ life in the mountains, eating only rice and drinking water.
definition: Characterized by or suggesting the practice of severe self-discipline and abstention from all forms of indulgence.

question: The detective was ______ in his investigation, refusing to overlook a single clue.
definition: Showing great care and perseverance.

question: Immigrants often try to ______ into the culture of their new country.
definition: Take in (information, ideas, or culture) and understand fully.

question: Taking aspirin helped to ______ the severe headache she was suffering from.
definition: Make an unpleasant feeling less intense.

question: The ______ detective solved the murder case in less than 24 hours.
definition: Having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations or people and turn this to one's advantage.

question: The long war became a battle of ______, with each side trying to wear the other down.
definition: The action or process of gradually reducing the strength or effectiveness of someone or something through sustained attack or pressure.

question: I couldn't believe he had the ______ to ask for a raise after arriving late all week.
definition: The willingness to take bold risks; rude or disrespectful behavior.

question: He took a second job at night to ______ his regular income.
definition: Make something greater by adding to it; increase.

question: Finding a four-leaf clover was seen as an ______ start to their journey.
definition: Conducive to success; favorable.

question: The ______ prison cell contained nothing but a hard cot and a toilet.
definition: Severe or strict in manner, attitude, or appearance; having no comforts or luxuries.

question: The dictator was an ______ ruler who demanded absolute obedience from his citizens.
definition: Taking no account of other people's wishes or opinions; domineering.

question: The province demanded to become ______, making its own laws separate from the capital.
definition: Having the freedom to govern itself or control its own affairs.

question: His ______ led him to steal millions of dollars from his own employees.
definition: Extreme greed for wealth or material gain.

question: The captured spy finally decided to ______ his true identity to the interrogators.
definition: Assert or confess openly.

question: The kindly old teacher treated his students with ______ affection.
definition: Kind and friendly toward a younger or less experienced person.

question: Our picnic plans went ______ when it suddenly started to pour down rain.
definition: Away from the appropriate, planned, or expected course; amiss.

question: It is a widely accepted ______ that what goes up must come down.
definition: A statement or proposition that is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true.

question: The pop song was incredibly ______, with predictable lyrics and a boring melody.
definition: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.

question: Mosquitoes are the ______ of my existence during the humid summer months.
definition: A cause of great distress or annoyance.

question: The university was seen as a ______ of free speech and progressive thinking.
definition: An institution, place, or person strongly defending or upholding particular principles.

question: The dictator's harsh laws will only ______ more rebellion among the citizens.
definition: Give rise to; bring about.

question: We all understood the point, so there was no need to ______ the issue further.
definition: Argue or elaborate a subject in excessive detail.

question: The enemy troops sought to ______ the castle by surrounding it completely.
definition: Lay siege to; beset with difficulties.

question: Her calm smile seemed to ______ the extreme panic she was actually feeling.
definition: Fail to give a true notion or impression of something; disguise or contradict.

question: The bully loved to ______ the smaller children to make himself feel powerful.
definition: Make someone or something seem unimportant.

question: The ______ drunk started picking fights with everyone in the bar.
definition: Hostile and aggressive.

question: I was completely ______ by the complex instructions on the exam.
definition: Puzzled, confused, or bewildered.

question: An anonymous ______ donated new computers to the entire school.
definition: A person who gives money or other help to a person or cause.

question: The ______ king gave land and gold to the poor farmers.
definition: Generous or doing good.

question: The doctor smiled and told her the tumor was ______, not cancerous.
definition: Gentle; kindly; not harmful in effect.

question: He received a large ______ of money in his grandfather's will.
definition: A legacy; the property or money that you promise in your will to give to someone after you die.

question: The ______ widow wept loudly at her husband's funeral.
definition: Deprived of a close relation or friend through their death.

question: The explorer's journey was ______ with danger, from wild animals to treacherous weather.
definition: Trouble or threaten persistently.

question: Screaming insults inside the church was considered pure ______.
definition: The act or offense of speaking sacrilegiously about God or sacred things; profane talk.

question: He told a ______ lie, claiming he was at work when we saw him at the beach.
definition: Done openly and unashamedly; very obvious.

question: The abandoned, ruined factories were a ______ on the city's landscape.
definition: A thing that spoils or damages something.

question: She showed a ______ disregard for the rules, walking right past the 'Do Not Enter' sign.
definition: Showing a casual and cheerful indifference considered to be callous or improper.

question: The artist hated the ______ lifestyle, preferring to live free from suburban norms.
definition: Of or characteristic of the middle class, typically with reference to its perceived materialistic values.

question: The slow-moving, heavy man had an almost ______ appearance as he chewed his food.
definition: Relating to or affecting cattle; sluggish or stupid.

question: The ______ of the CEO's speech was appreciated by the tired employees.
definition: Concise and exact use of words in writing or speech; shortness of time.

question: I didn't know how to ______ the subject of a raise with my strict boss.
definition: Raise a sensitive or difficult subject for discussion.

question: The painting depicted a calm, ______ scene of sheep grazing in a green meadow.
definition: Relating to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life.

question: Getting a new passport involves dealing with endless government ______ and paperwork.
definition: A system of government in which most of the important decisions are made by state officials rather than by elected representatives.

question: The comedian's ______ of the president had the audience roaring with laughter.
definition: An absurd or comically exaggerated imitation of something, especially in a literary or dramatic work.

question: The ______ young soldier had never seen combat before.
definition: Inexperienced and immature.

question: I appreciated her ______, as she told me the honest truth even when it hurt.
definition: The quality of being open and honest in expression; frankness.

question: Under the pressure of the siege, the city was eventually forced to ______.
definition: Cease to resist an opponent or an unwelcome demand; surrender.

question: The street artist drew a funny ______ of me with a giant head and tiny body.
definition: A picture, description, or imitation of a person in which certain striking characteristics are exaggerated in order to create a comic or grotesque effect.

question: The principal decided to ______ the boys publicly for vandalizing the lockers.
definition: Reprimand someone severely.

question: The assassination of the archduke served as the ______ for World War I.
definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without itself undergoing any permanent chemical change; a person or thing that precipitates an event.

question: The CEO issued a ______ denial of all the fraud allegations.
definition: Unambiguously explicit and direct.

question: Screaming into a pillow can be a great emotional ______ for relieving stress.
definition: The process of releasing, and thereby providing relief from, strong or repressed emotions.

question: Her musical tastes are very ______, ranging from classical opera to heavy metal.
definition: Including a wide variety of things; all-embracing.

question: His ______ sarcastic remarks offended everyone at the dinner table.
definition: Able to burn or corrode organic tissue by chemical action; sarcastic in a scathing and bitter way.

question: The monk took a vow of ______ when he entered the monastery.
definition: The state of abstaining from marriage and sexual relations.

question: The corrupt politician received a formal ______ from the ethics committee.
definition: Express severe disapproval of someone or something, especially in a formal statement.

question: Chess is a highly ______ game that requires deep thinking and strategy.
definition: Intellectual rather than emotional or physical.

question: Much to his ______, he realized he had worn mismatched shoes to the job interview.
definition: Distress or embarrassment at having failed or been humiliated.

question: The cult leader possessed a dark ______ that drew vulnerable people to him.
definition: Compelling attractiveness or charm that can inspire devotion in others.

question: The traveling salesman was a ______ who sold fake medicine made of sugar water.
definition: A person falsely claiming to have a special knowledge or skill; a fraud.

question: The earthquake opened a deep ______ in the middle of the highway.
definition: A deep fissure in the earth, rock, or another surface.

question: The mother had to ______ her son for running out into the street without looking.
definition: Rebuke or reprimand severely.

question: The shady lawyer used legal ______ to swindle the old woman out of her money.
definition: The use of trickery to achieve a political, financial, or legal purpose.

question: His hope of becoming a billionaire overnight was nothing but a foolish ______.
definition: A thing that is hoped or wished for but in fact is illusory or impossible to achieve.

question: The ______ old man yelled at anyone who accidentally stepped on his lawn.
definition: Bad-tempered or irritable.

question: She suffered from ______ back pain that lasted for years without stopping.
definition: Persisting for a long time or constantly recurring.

question: The historian wrote a detailed ______ of the ancient Roman empire's rise and fall.
definition: A factual written account of important or historical events in the order of their occurrence.

question: Because the main road was flooded, we had to take a ______ route through the mountains.
definition: Longer than the most direct way.

question: The politician's ______ made it obvious he didn't want to answer the direct question.
definition: The use of many words where fewer would do, especially in a deliberate attempt to be vague or evasive.

question: The electric fence was built to ______ the pasture and keep the wolves out.
definition: Restrict something within limits.

question: A ______ investor will thoroughly research a company before buying its stock.
definition: Wary and unwilling to take risks.

question: The hackers found a way to ______ the bank's security firewall.
definition: Find a way around an obstacle; overcome a problem or difficulty, typically in a clever and surreptitious way.

question: Even though they were divorced, they managed to be ______ to each other for their kids' sake.
definition: Courteous and polite.

question: The prisoner begged the governor for ______, hoping to avoid the death penalty.
definition: Mercy; lenience.

question: The romance movie was full of every ______, from running in the rain to stopping a wedding.
definition: A phrase or opinion that is overused and betrays a lack of original thought.

question: The popular girls in high school formed an exclusive ______ that ignored everyone else.
definition: A small group of people, with shared interests or other features in common, who spend time together and do not readily allow others to join them.

question: The scattered drops of water began to ______ into a single large puddle.
definition: Come together and form one mass or whole.

question: The mafia tried to ______ the witness into lying by threatening his family.
definition: Persuade an unwilling person to do something by using force or threats.

question: The lawyer presented a ______ argument that convinced the jury of his client's innocence.
definition: Clear, logical, and convincing.

question: The puzzle was designed to test the ______ abilities of young children.
definition: Relating to cognition; the mental action or process of acquiring knowledge and understanding.

question: A good driver must be ______ of their surroundings at all times.
definition: Having knowledge or being aware of.

question: The drunk man could not form a ______ sentence when the police questioned him.
definition: Logical and consistent; united as or forming a whole.

question: The novel was written in a ______ style, using everyday slang and casual language.
definition: Used in ordinary or familiar conversation; not formal or literary.

question: The rival gas stations acted in ______ to artificially raise the price of fuel.
definition: Secret or illegal cooperation or conspiracy, especially in order to cheat or deceive others.

question: Your salary will be ______ with your level of experience and education.
definition: Corresponding in size or degree; in proportion.

question: The documentary offered a ______ look at the devastating effects of climate change.
definition: Evoking interest, attention, or admiration in a powerfully irresistible way.

question: He published a massive ______ of all known insect species in the Amazon.
definition: A collection of concise but detailed information about a particular subject.

question: A nice red wine will perfectly ______ the steak dinner.
definition: A thing that completes or brings to perfection.

question: The getaway driver was charged with ______ in the bank robbery.
definition: The state of being involved with others in an illegal activity or wrongdoing.

question: The final exam will be ______, covering everything we learned this semester.
definition: Complete; including all or nearly all elements or aspects of something.

question: The United States is ______ of fifty individual states.
definition: Consist of; be made up of.

question: He sent her a bouquet of flowers as a ______ gesture after their big fight.
definition: Intended or likely to placate or pacify.

question: Please be ______ in your presentation; we only have five minutes.
definition: Giving a lot of information clearly and in a few words; brief but comprehensive.

question: The DNA test provided ______ proof that he was the child's father.
definition: Serving to prove a case; decisive or convincing.

question: The chef managed to ______ a delicious soup out of leftover vegetables.
definition: Make a dish or meal by combining various ingredients; create or devise a story or plan.

question: Deafness is a frequent ______ symptom of advanced old age.
definition: Naturally accompanying or associated.

question: The two warring nations finally signed a treaty of ______ and peace.
definition: Agreement or harmony between people or groups.

question: I completely ______ with your assessment of the situation; you are absolutely right.
definition: Be of the same opinion; agree.

question: The arrogant professor would never ______ to speak to an undergraduate student.
definition: Show feelings of superiority; be patronizing.

question: The school absolutely does not ______ cheating on exams under any circumstances.
definition: Accept and allow behavior that is considered morally wrong or offensive to continue.

question: A quiet room is highly ______ to studying and retaining information.
definition: Making a certain situation or outcome likely or possible.

question: The city of Pittsburgh sits at the ______ of three major rivers.
definition: The junction of two rivers, especially rivers of approximately equal width.

question: The ______ host made sure that every guest at the dinner party felt perfectly comfortable.
definition: Pleasant because of a personality, qualities, or interests that are similar to one's own.

question: The baby was born with a ______ heart defect that required immediate surgery.
definition: Present from birth.

question: The fans began to ______ outside the stadium hours before the concert started.
definition: Gather into a crowd or mass.

question: Without any hard evidence, the detective's theory was nothing but pure ______.
definition: An opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete information.

question: The witch chanted a spell to ______ a demon from the underworld.
definition: Make something appear unexpectedly or seemingly from nowhere as if by magic.

question: He is a ______ of fine wines, able to identify the vintage just by smelling it.
definition: An expert judge in matters of taste.

question: The bishop arrived to ______ the new church building, making it holy ground.
definition: Make or declare sacred; dedicate formally to a religious or divine purpose.

question: The jury debated for hours before finally reaching a ______ on the verdict.
definition: A general agreement.

question: His actions were simply not ______ with the promises he made during the campaign.
definition: In agreement or harmony with.

question: His silence could easily be ______ as an admission of guilt.
definition: Interpret a word or action in a particular way.

question: He was a ______ professional, handling the difficult client with perfect grace and skill.
definition: Showing a high degree of skill and flair; complete or perfect.

question: Abortion remains a highly ______ issue in American politics.
definition: Causing or likely to cause an argument; controversial.

question: The United States and Canada share a massive ______ border.
definition: Sharing a common border; touching.

question: The outdoor wedding is completely ______ on the weather being sunny.
definition: Subject to chance; occurring or existing only if certain other circumstances are the case.

question: The ______ thief apologized to the family he had stolen from and returned the money.
definition: Feeling or expressing remorse or penitence; affected by guilt.

question: The movie's ending felt very ______, as if the writers just threw it together at the last second.
definition: Deliberately created rather than arising naturally or spontaneously.

question: He wore a ______ black tuxedo to the formal event, blending in with everyone else.
definition: Based on or in accordance with what is generally done or believed.

question: The ______ atmosphere at the pub made everyone feel like old friends.
definition: Friendly, lively, and enjoyable.

question: She took ______ notes during the lecture, filling up an entire notebook.
definition: Abundant in supply or quantity.

question: A higher crime rate is often the inevitable ______ of rising poverty levels.
definition: A proposition that follows from one already proved; a direct or natural consequence or result.

question: The witness stepped forward to ______ the suspect's alibi, proving he wasn't at the scene.
definition: Confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding.

question: London is a highly ______ city, filled with people and cultures from all over the world.
definition: Familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures.

question: I cannot ______ your decision to drop out of high school.
definition: Admit as acceptable or possible.

question: The military staged a violent ______ and overthrew the democratic government.
definition: Not openly acknowledged or displayed; secret.

question: The CIA agent was sent on a highly ______ mission behind enemy lines.
definition: Not openly acknowledged or displayed.

question: Thou shalt not ______ thy neighbor's wife or possessions.
definition: Yearn to possess or have something.

question: The ______ soldier abandoned his post the moment he heard the enemy approaching.
definition: Contemptibly lacking in courage; cowardly.

question: Scammers often target ______ individuals who believe everything they are told.
definition: Having or showing too great a readiness to believe things.

question: The music built to a dramatic ______, with every instrument playing at maximum volume.
definition: A gradual increase in loudness in a piece of music; the highest point of intensity.

question: Years of hard work and training will ______ in her competing at the Olympic games.
definition: Reach a climax or point of highest development.

question: The corrupt politician's ______ led him to accept bribes from wealthy developers.
definition: Greed for money or possessions.

question: The neighborhood ______ yelled at the kids to get off his perfectly manicured lawn.
definition: A bad-tempered or surly person.

question: He has a ______ view of the world, believing everyone is only out for themselves.
definition: Believing that people are motivated by self-interest; distrustful of human sincerity.

question: The product launch was a complete ______, with servers crashing and negative reviews pouring in.
definition: A sudden and ignominious failure; a fiasco.

question: The severe illness will ______ him for several months, requiring complete bed rest.
definition: Make someone very weak and infirm.

question: The scientist went on television to ______ the popular myth about brain capacity.
definition: Expose the falseness or hollowness of a myth, idea, or belief.

question: Students are expected to behave with ______ during the graduation ceremony.
definition: Behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety.

question: Smoking has a highly ______ effect on lung capacity and overall health.
definition: Causing harm or damage.

question: The architect used a red marker to ______ the boundaries of the new property on the map.
definition: Describe or portray something precisely; indicate the exact position of a border.

question: The ______ rose to power by appealing to the fears and prejudices of the voters.
definition: A political leader who seeks support by appealing to popular desires and prejudices rather than by using rational argument.

question: It is unfair to ______ her hard work just because you disagree with her methods.
definition: Criticize unfairly; disparage.

question: The serial killer's actions showed a level of moral ______ that shocked the entire nation.
definition: Moral corruption; wickedness.

question: The software company will ______ the old app and replace it with a new, faster version.
definition: Express disapproval of; (of a software feature) be usable but regarded as obsolete.

question: The critics were quick to ______ the director's latest film as a complete waste of time.
definition: Express contempt for; ridicule.

question: Her art style was highly ______, copying heavily from Picasso without adding anything new.
definition: Imitative of the work of another person, and usually disapproved of for that reason.

question: The hot desert sun will quickly ______ any plant that isn't adapted to drought conditions.
definition: Remove the moisture from something; cause to become completely dry.

question: After failing the bar exam for the third time, he felt completely ______.
definition: In low spirits from loss of hope or courage.

question: The country was ruled by a ruthless ______ who imprisoned anyone who opposed him.
definition: A ruler or other person who holds absolute power, typically one who exercises it in a cruel or oppressive way.

question: The earthquake left thousands of families ______, without food, money, or shelter.
definition: Without the basic necessities of life.

question: She made a ______ attempt to clean her room, picking up two shirts and then giving up.
definition: Lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm.

question: The high fence and security cameras are meant to ______ trespassers.
definition: Discourage someone from doing something by instilling doubt or fear of the consequences.

question: The ugly scratch on the hood does not ______ from the overall performance of the car.
definition: Diminish the worth or value of a quality or achievement.

question: Eating too much sugar is ______ to your dental health.
definition: Tending to cause harm.

question: The villain used ______ tactics to trick the hero into a trap.
definition: Showing a skillful use of underhanded tactics to achieve goals.

question: The coach launched into a furious ______ against the referee's unfair calls.
definition: A forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something.

question: Children's books are often ______, designed to teach a moral lesson along with the story.
definition: Intended to teach, particularly in having moral instruction as an ulterior motive.

question: The ______ young boy rarely spoke in class and avoided making eye contact.
definition: Modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence.

question: I don't mean to ______, but that reminds me of a funny story from my childhood.
definition: Leave the main subject temporarily in speech or writing.

question: The homeowner used ______ tactics to delay paying the contractor for the roof repair.
definition: Slow to act; intended to cause delay.

question: He's just a ______ when it comes to photography, rarely touching his expensive camera.
definition: A person who cultivates an area of interest, such as the arts, without real commitment or knowledge.

question: The choir sang a slow, mournful ______ at the soldier's funeral.
definition: A lament for the dead, especially one forming part of a funeral rite.

question: I must ______ you of the notion that starting a business is easy and stress-free.
definition: Persuade someone that an idea or belief is mistaken.

question: It was difficult to ______ any truth in his chaotic and rambling story.
definition: Perceive or recognize something.

question: The beginner orchestra produced a highly ______ sound as they tried to tune their instruments.
definition: Disagreeing or incongruous; harsh and jarring because of a lack of harmony.

question: The opposing political party tried to ______ the mayor by leaking his private emails.
definition: Harm the good reputation of someone or something.

question: There was a major ______ between the witness's testimony and the video footage.
definition: A lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts.

question: The seminar was broken down into five ______ sessions, each covering a different topic.
definition: Individually separate and distinct.

question: The city is made up of ______ cultures that rarely interact with one another.
definition: Essentially different in kind; not allowing comparison.

question: An honest person will find it very difficult to ______ their true feelings.
definition: Conceal one's true motives, feelings, or beliefs.

question: The internet makes it incredibly easy to ______ information globally in seconds.
definition: Spread something, especially information, widely.

question: The severe ______ between her religious beliefs and her actions caused her great guilt.
definition: A tension or clash resulting from the combination of two inharmonious or unsuitable elements.

question: Malnutrition can cause a child's stomach to ______ unnaturally.
definition: Swell or cause to swell by pressure from inside.

question: The two friends' career paths began to ______ after they graduated from college.
definition: Separate from another route, especially a main one, and go in a different direction.

question: He was highly ______ about his political views, refusing to listen to any counterarguments.
definition: Inclined to lay down principles as incontrovertibly true.

question: The volcano has been ______ for centuries, but scientists fear it may erupt soon.
definition: Having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period of time; in or as if in a deep sleep.

question: The con artist managed to ______ several elderly people into giving him their credit card numbers.
definition: Deceive; trick.

question: His ______ was finally exposed when both of his secret families found out about each other.
definition: Deceitfulness; double-dealing.

question: Her music taste is highly ______, featuring everything from jazz to heavy metal.
definition: Deriving ideas, style, or taste from a broad and diverse range of sources.

question: The pastor delivered a sermon meant to ______ the congregation and encourage better morals.
definition: Instruct or improve someone morally or intellectually.

question: Over hundreds of years, the wind and rain began to ______ the writing on the tombstone.
definition: Erase a mark from a surface; make oneself appear insignificant or inconspicuous.

question: Her ______ personality made her the life of every party she attended.
definition: Vivacious and enthusiastic; giving off bubbles.

question: The new vaccine was proven to be highly ______ in preventing the spread of the virus.
definition: Successful in producing a desired or intended result; effective.

question: I can't believe he had the ______ to ask for a raise after being caught stealing.
definition: Insolent or impertinent behavior.

question: The poet read a beautiful ______ at his best friend's memorial service.
definition: A poem of serious reflection, typically a lament for the dead.

question: The detective tried to ______ a confession from the suspect by offering a lighter sentence.
definition: Evoke or draw out a response, answer, or fact from someone.

question: The textbook used helpful diagrams to ______ the complex physics concepts.
definition: Make something clear; explain.

question: The snow leopard is an ______ creature, rarely seen by human eyes in the wild.
definition: Difficult to find, catch, or achieve.

question: He tends to ______ his stories, adding dragons and explosions to things that never happened.
definition: Make something more attractive by the addition of decorative details or features; exaggerate.

question: The scientist demanded ______ evidence, refusing to rely on theories and guesswork.
definition: Based on, concerned with, or verifiable by observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic.

question: Malaria is ______ to certain tropical regions, meaning it is regularly found there.
definition: Regularly found among particular people or in a certain area.

question: The intense humidity of the jungle will quickly ______ an unprepared hiker.
definition: Cause someone to feel drained of energy or vitality; weaken.

question: The mayor's controversial comments will certainly ______ a lot of anger in the community.
definition: Cause or give rise to a feeling, situation, or condition.

question: The wealthy heir suffered from extreme ______, finding no joy or excitement in his luxurious life.
definition: A feeling of listlessness and dissatisfaction arising from a lack of occupation or excitement.

question: As a true ______, she traveled to France simply to taste the region's famous cheeses.
definition: A person who takes particular pleasure in fine food and drink.

question: The airline pilot handled the engine failure with perfect ______, safely landing the plane without panic.
definition: Mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper, especially in a difficult situation.

question: When asked if he stole the money, the suspect began to ______ rather than give a straight answer.
definition: Use ambiguous language so as to conceal the truth or avoid committing oneself.

question: The stock market's ______ behavior made investors incredibly nervous.
definition: Not even or regular in pattern or movement; unpredictable.

question: The ______ professor could fluently read Latin, Greek, and ancient Sumerian.
definition: Having or showing great knowledge or learning.

question: She is an ______ scientist whose research has saved countless lives.
definition: Worthy of great respect.

question: The president delivered a moving ______ at the funeral of the fallen general.
definition: A speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly, typically someone who has just died.

question: Saying someone 'passed away' is a common ______ for saying they died.
definition: A mild or indirect word or expression substituted for one considered to be too harsh or blunt.

question: The security camera footage will ______ the defendant, proving he wasn't at the bank.
definition: Show or declare that someone is not guilty of wrongdoing.

question: The hotel room was in ______ condition, with mold on the walls and cockroaches on the floor.
definition: Extremely bad or unpleasant.

question: The patient's failing heart condition created an ______ need for a transplant.
definition: Pressing; demanding.

question: The DNA results finally ______ the man who had been falsely accused of the crime.
definition: Absolve someone from blame for a fault or wrongdoing.

question: The theme park charged an ______ price for a simple bottle of water.
definition: Unreasonably high in price or amount.

question: The professor loves to ______ on the history of ancient Rome, talking for hours without stopping.
definition: Speak or write at length or in detail.

question: Many American writers lived as ______ in Paris during the 1920s.
definition: A person who lives outside their native country.

question: Given the approaching storm, it is ______ that we leave the island immediately.
definition: Convenient and practical, although possibly improper or immoral.

question: He volunteered at the homeless shelter to ______ for his past selfish behavior.
definition: Atone for guilt or sin.

question: The teacher gave ______ instructions, telling the students exactly what to do step-by-step.
definition: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion or doubt.

question: After the pardon, the judge ordered the clerk to ______ the crime from the man's permanent record.
definition: Erase or remove completely something unwanted or unpleasant.

question: The school board decided to ______ the novel, removing the curse words before giving it to students.
definition: Remove matter thought to be objectionable or unsuitable from a book or account.

question: There are only three ______ copies of this 16th-century map left in the world.
definition: Still in existence; surviving.

question: The judge gave a lighter sentence due to the ______ circumstances surrounding the theft.
definition: Serving to lessen the seriousness of an offence.

question: The health magazine continues to ______ the benefits of a plant-based diet.
definition: Praise enthusiastically.

question: Please keep your presentation focused and remove any ______ details that waste time.
definition: Irrelevant or unrelated to the subject being dealt with.

question: Based on current sales, we can ______ that revenue will double by next year.
definition: Extend the application of a method or conclusion to an unknown situation by assuming existing trends will continue.

question: The firefighters had to use the jaws of life to ______ the driver from the crushed car.
definition: Free someone or something from a constraint or difficulty.

question: The puppy was naturally ______, bouncing around the room and licking everyone's faces.
definition: Filled with or characterized by a lively energy and excitement.

question: The employee had to ______ a complicated lie to explain why he was late to work.
definition: Invent or concoct something, typically with deceitful intent.

question: His essay provided a very ______ explanation of the war, completely ignoring the complex economic causes.
definition: Appearing neat and comprehensive only by ignoring the true complexities of an issue; superficial.

question: He was ______ about cleanliness, scrubbing his floors with a toothbrush every night.
definition: Filled with excessive and single-minded zeal.

question: I simply cannot ______ why anyone would want to jump out of a perfectly good airplane.
definition: Understand a difficult problem or an enigmatic person after much thought.

question: Asking if the sun is hot is a perfectly ______ question.
definition: Silly and pointless.

question: The celebrity was surrounded by an entourage of ______ assistants who agreed with his every word.
definition: Displaying exaggerated flattery or affection; obsequious.
